chunk_summary: This section outlines empiric antibiotic regimens for severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing protocolized treatment based on complication status, with specific dosages and durations for various conditions.
learning_objectives: Understand the rationale for empiric antibiotic use in SAM due to silent infections and blunted inflammatory responses.
Identify first-line antibiotic regimens for uncomplicated and complicated SAM cases.
Learn the criteria for escalating therapy and modifying antibiotics based on clinical response or culture results.
Recognize syndrome-specific durations for antibiotic therapy in SAM-related infections.
key_facts: Uncomplicated SAM: Oral amoxicillin 15 mg/kg every 8 hours for 5 days.
Complicated SAM: Inj. ampicillin 50 mg/kg every 6 hours + inj. gentamicin 7.5 mg/kg once daily for 7 days.
Suspected staphylococcal infection: Add inj. cloxacillin (100 mg/kg/day in divided doses).
Sepsis treatment duration: 7 days for suspected, 10–14 days for culture-proven cases.
Meningitis treatment duration: 14–21 days; deep-seated infections require at least 4 weeks.
Third-generation cephalosporins (e.g., cefotaxime 150 mg/kg/day or ceftriaxone 100 mg/kg/day) are used for severe cases or specific syndromes.
reference_quote: In SAM, routine infections can be clinically silent and inflammatory responses blunted, so empiric antibiotics are protocolized rather than optional.
document_key_topics: Management of uncomplicated and complicated SAM
Criteria for therapy escalation and modification
